key_insight: A variable-offset joint can couple beams of arbitrary cross-section without explicit rotational interface variables by enforcing local configurational constraints in a moving frame and eliminating multipliers with a null-space method.
engineering_relevance: This enables more general, numerically efficient beam-to-beam and beam-to-solid connection modeling in nonlinear finite element analysis, especially when standard joint formulations are not compatible with the chosen kinematics.
method: Define offset coordinates in a moving frame to constrain or release relative beam positions
Approximate the Lagrange multiplier field in the cross-section with first-order terms
Eliminate multipliers using a discrete null-space method to reduce system size and improve conditioning
evidence_signal: The abstract reports application to both an extensible-director beam element and a brick element in nonlinear elastostatics, with several numerical examples demonstrating the formulation.
watch_item: Because the multiplier field is only first-order in the cross-section, the joint transfer is limited to resultant force and moment, which may restrict fidelity for highly local connection effects.
paper_title: A variable-offset joint formulation for beams with arbitrary cross-sections using a null space method
